diagrams.title: C++ Function Anatomy and Call Flow
diagrams.description: Shows the structure of a C++ function including return type, function name, parameters, and body, along with the flow of execution when a function is called from main() and returns a value.
elements: Arrow showing call flow from main to function
Arrow showing return flow with value
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental structure of C++ functions and how data flows through them, helping learners understand function invocation and return mechanisms.

diagrams.title: Type Casting in C++
diagrams.description: Illustrates the difference between implicit (automatic) type casting and explicit type casting in C++, including the four C++ casting operators: static_cast, dynamic_cast, const_cast, and reinterpret_cast.
elements: Data type hierarchy (char → int → float → double)
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies the distinction between implicit and explicit type conversions, and introduces the type-safe C++ casting operators for proper type handling.

diagrams.title: Type Conversion Safety Examples
diagrams.description: Provides visual examples of safe vs unsafe type conversions in C++, showing common scenarios like int to double (safe), double to int (potential data loss), and pointer type casting with proper casting operators.
elements: Base class to derived class casting
void pointer to typed pointer casting
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates practical examples of type casting scenarios, helping programmers understand when conversions are safe and when they require explicit casts or caution.
